title: OpenAI Merges ChatGPT and Codex Into One Unified Agentic Platform
summary: OpenAI president Greg Brockman is permanently leading product strategy after merging ChatGPT, Codex, and the developer API into a single agentic platform ahead of the company's IPO.
content: OpenAI co-founder and president Greg Brockman has permanently taken charge of the company's product strategy, merging ChatGPT, Codex, and the developer API into a single product organisation. In an internal memo seen by Wired, Brockman wrote that OpenAI will 'invest in a single agentic platform and to merge ChatGPT and Codex into one unified agentic experience for all.' The restructuring is partly IPO-facing — OpenAI is preparing to go public in Q4 2026 at a valuation of approximately $852 billion, and a single-platform narrative is simpler to pitch to institutional investors. The unified platform, internally called an agentic 'super app,' will allow users to converse, write code, execute multi-step tasks, browse the web, manage files, and interact with external services all within one interface. Codex will expand first to cover productivity tasks beyond coding before ChatGPT and the research tool Atlas are folded in.

title: EU Strikes Deal to Delay AI Act High-Risk Rules Until December 2027
summary: European Parliament and Council negotiators agreed to postpone key high-risk AI compliance deadlines and exempt many industrial applications from the scope of the law.
content: Restrictions on high-risk uses of artificial intelligence in the EU will be postponed by more than a year under a deal agreed by EU legislators, pushing the compliance deadline for most high-risk AI systems from August 2026 to December 2027. The deal largely exempts AI in industrial applications like machinery from the AI Act's direct scope — a significant win for Germany after pressure from Siemens and Bosch — and delays watermarking obligations for AI-generated content to December 2026. The agreement also bans AI systems used to create 'nudifier' content or child sexual abuse material, with a compliance deadline of December 2026. Commission President Ursula von der Leyen said the deal provides 'a simple, innovation-friendly environment' for AI in Europe. The rollback marks the first significant retreat from digital regulation in the EU, driven by US pressure and warnings from European industry that strict AI rules were hampering competitiveness in the global AI race.

title: Google Launches Gemini Intelligence on Android With Deep Agentic Automation
summary: Google announced Gemini Intelligence for Android at its pre-I/O showcase, bringing cross-app task automation, AI-powered Chrome browsing, and a natural language widget builder to premium Galaxy and Pixel phones this summer.
content: Google announced Gemini Intelligence for Android, enabling Gemini to automate multi-step tasks across apps — for example, copying a grocery list from a notes app and adding items to a shopping cart, using the phone's screen context as input. Starting in late June, Android devices will receive Gemini in Chrome for summarization, comparison, and form autofill powered by users' Google Photos and Gmail data through a 'Personal Intelligence' connection. A new Rambler feature for Gboard transcribes natural speech into polished text, while 'Create My Widget' lets users build custom home screen widgets by describing their desired functionality in plain language. Google executive Sameer Samat described the shift as 'transitioning from an operating system to an intelligence system,' with Gemini confirming with the user before completing any transaction. Features will roll out first to Samsung Galaxy and Google Pixel phones this summer, then expand to watches, cars, glasses, and laptops.

title: OpenAI Releases GPT-5.5-Cyber for Critical Infrastructure Defenders
summary: OpenAI launched a limited preview of GPT-5.5-Cyber, a specialized AI model for cybersecurity teams protecting critical infrastructure, requiring stricter identity verification than standard models.
content: OpenAI has rolled out GPT-5.5-Cyber in limited preview to defenders responsible for securing critical infrastructure, supporting specialized cybersecurity workflows that require more permissive behavior than the general-purpose GPT-5.5 model allows. The model incorporates the Trusted Access for Cyber (TAC) framework, which enforces identity and trust-based access policies, and will require phishing-resistant account security from all users starting June 1, 2026. During alpha testing, GPT-5.5-Cyber was already used to scale automated red-teaming of critical systems and validate high-severity vulnerabilities, with findings to be documented in a future technical deep-dive as part of responsible disclosure. Strategic partners for the rollout include Cisco, CrowdStrike, Palo Alto Networks, Oracle, Zscaler, Cloudflare, Akamai, and Fortinet. OpenAI emphasized that GPT-5.5 with standard Trusted Access for Cyber remains the recommended starting point for most security workflows, while GPT-5.5-Cyber supports more specialized authorized use cases with stronger verification and misuse monitoring.

title: Anthropic Makes Claude Platform Natively Available on AWS for Enterprise Developers
summary: The Claude Platform on AWS is now generally available, giving AWS customers direct access to Anthropic's full native API — including Claude Managed Agents, MCP connectors, and all day-one beta features — through their existing AWS account.
content: Anthropic has launched the Claude Platform on AWS as generally available, making AWS the first cloud provider to offer the native Claude Platform experience, with all new features and betas shipping the same day as the native Claude API. Developers can access Claude Managed Agents, web search, code execution, a Files API, Skills, MCP connectors, prompt caching, citations, and batch processing directly through their existing AWS account. Unlike Claude on Amazon Bedrock — where AWS is the data processor and data stays within the AWS boundary — the Claude Platform on AWS is operated by Anthropic, suiting teams that want full-feature access without strict regional data residency requirements. The service supports IAM-based authentication, consolidated AWS billing, and CloudTrail audit logging. Claude Opus 4.7, Sonnet 4.6, and Haiku 4.5 are available at launch, with new models shipping on the Claude Platform on AWS on the same day they release on the native API.

title: Google Blocks First Known Zero-Day Exploit Developed With AI Assistance
summary: Google's Threat Intelligence Group identified and disrupted a zero-day exploit likely built with AI, marking the first confirmed instance of AI-assisted attack code discovered and stopped in the wild.
content: Google researchers from the Threat Intelligence Group (GTIG) disrupted what they say is the first zero-day exploit developed with the help of AI, flagged by evidence including a 'hallucinated' CVSS score and 'structured, textbook' formatting consistent with LLM-generated code in the Python exploit script. The exploit targeted a logic flaw in the two-factor authentication system of an unnamed open-source web-based administration tool, and was intended for a 'mass exploitation event' by prominent cybercriminal threat actors. Google said it was able to disrupt this particular attack but warned that hackers are using AI to find and exploit vulnerabilities at what a separate GTIG report called 'industrial scale.' The report also details 'persona-driven jailbreaking' techniques used by attackers to extract vulnerability research from AI models, and how they are feeding entire repositories of vulnerability data to AI systems to generate more reliable exploits. Google researchers noted that they 'do not believe Gemini was used' to create this specific exploit.
